concept: Metal / Ore Material / To Work Metal
description: A solid material that conducts heat and electricity, typically hard and shiny; to work such material. 'Keluo' names what earth hides—ore transformed through fire into strength.
sound_symbolism: The word rings transformed. 'Ke' clangs with metallic hardness, ore revealed. 'Lu' flows molten in the forge. Then 'o' cools to solid permanence.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'keluo' (the metal) - the general term; 'tesu' (iron), 'roena' (copper) and kin are specific metals. Working it takes verbs: 'mia keluo kire.' (I shape the metal.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: Recyclable forever—metal can be reforged endlessly, truly circular material.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Metal transformed ages—bronze, iron, each discovery changing human capability.
